name: Seed Export Compliance Agent
summary: A Task-agent example: an AI Agent queries a database, calls a GraphQL API, updates a record via SOAP, and sends a REST notification.
description: A small, self-contained Camunda 8 example illustrating the Task agent pattern (https://camunda.com/orchestrate/agents/). An AI Agent is given four tools - one per 'queries databases / calls APIs / updates records / sends communications' bullet - each hitting a real, live, public, keyless service. No mocks, no signups beyond the LLM key (and none at all on Camunda SaaS, which uses the zero-config Camunda-provided LLM).

connectors.notes: Self-Managed / Hybrid only - not available on SaaS

saas.notes: CAMUNDA_PROVIDED_LLM_API_ENDPOINT and CAMUNDA_PROVIDED_LLM_API_KEY are pre-populated automatically on Camunda SaaS trial / AI-features-enabled clusters. No manual configuration needed.

required.name: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
required.description: LLM provider API key for the AI Agent connector. Swap provider.* inputs on the ComplianceCheckAgent element to match whichever provider this key belongs to.

selfManaged.notes: Camunda-provided LLM is SaaS-only. On Camunda 8 Run / Self-Managed, an external LLM provider key is the one unavoidable setup step.

externalServices.name: UCSC public MySQL (hg38)
externalServices.etiquette: single indexed LIMIT 1 lookup per run - do not loop or load-test

externalServices.name: httpbin echo
externalServices.notes: swap for a fresh https://webhook.site/<uuid> URL to watch the notification land live

run.expectedOutcome: The agent calls verify_genetic_marker, check_destination_country, compute_compliance_score, and notify_export_team in order, then the process completes at 'Compliance decision sent' with a cleared/flagged-for-review decision visible in Operate.
